## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)

On October 11, 1968, the incredibly massive, absolutely historic, and highly successful Apollo 7 mission officially and aggressively launched from Cape Kennedy. Heavily commanded by Wally Schirra, this massive orbital flight was the absolute, very first crewed mission of the Apollo program following the horrific, deeply tragic Apollo 1 fire. The incredibly massive, 11-day mission absolutely validated the redesigned Command Module, fiercely restoring NASA's absolute momentum and fundamentally paving the incredibly massive path for the impending lunar landings.



On October 11, 1899, the incredibly massive, heavily brutal, and absolutely defining Second Boer War officially and aggressively erupted in South Africa. The massive British Empire violently clashed with the fiercely independent Boer republics of the Transvaal and the Orange Free State over massive gold and diamond reserves. The incredibly bloody, three-year conflict absolutely decimated the massive Boer civilian population through the horrific, aggressive use of massive British concentration camps, fundamentally establishing total British colonial dominance over South Africa.
